notes: This metadata intentionally models channel-related fields as useCaseChannelRuleList instead of flat fields.
Business descriptions and answer guidance are preserved to support AI question hints.
Some items need later business confirmation: push_optin_flag semantic meaning, OTX_BATCH_LETTER vs OTX_BAT_LETTER, and exact PUSH DaaSC tag/route combination.

use_case_master.description: Top-level use case fields stored mainly in tbl_use_case.

use_case_extension.description: Ownership, business line, risk and trigger fields stored mainly in tbl_use_case_ext.

use_case_channel_rule_list.description: Repeated channel rule object. Each selected delivery channel may create one or more channel rule items with priority, router, route, tag, sender and routing strategy.

opt_in_policy.description: Opt-in master flag / generated consent indicator. Raw field name is push_optin_flag but business wording suggests master opt-in flag.

channel_priority.label: M
channel_priority.description: Business sheet includes M as equivalent/label for mandatory

router_catalog.note: Business note also mentions OTX_BATCH_LETTER; confirm whether it is the same value as OTX_BAT_LETTER.

question_groups.title: Basic use case context
question_groups.business_goal: Understand what the use case is and how messages are sent.

question_groups.title: Business trigger and risk
question_groups.business_goal: Understand when the message is triggered and whether it is high risk or 7*24.

question_groups.title: Delivery channel
question_groups.business_goal: Select the delivery channels and priority before asking channel-specific details.

question_groups.title: Routing and vendor path
question_groups.business_goal: Confirm router, route, tags and traffic split for selected channels.

question_groups.title: Bounce-back and fallback
question_groups.business_goal: Configure callback, failed/unknown status handling and next-channel fallback.

fields.business_description: Line of business.

answer_guidance.business_hint: Select the owning line of business for this use case.
answer_guidance.assistant_hint: Can be prefilled by checking department / user access information, then ask user to confirm only if uncertain.

fields.business_description: The business scenario for the message triggering. It is used for users to check the related message delivery information in MDC MI report.

answer_guidance.business_hint: Provide a short business scenario name that users can recognize in MDC MI report.
answer_guidance.assistant_hint: Generate a concise candidate name from requirement and ask user to confirm.

fields.business_description: Project name.

answer_guidance.business_hint: Provide the related project name.
answer_guidance.assistant_hint: Ask later if not found in requirement; do not interrupt core channel/trigger decisions early.

fields.business_description: Source system name that will pass the notification request to MDC.

answer_guidance.business_hint: Provide the upstream system that sends the notification request to MDC.
answer_guidance.assistant_hint: If missing, ask as an integration question after basic business scenario is clear.

fields.business_description: Delivery mode. Time Critical is for OTP only. Other values include Realtime and Batch.

answer_guidance.business_hint: Choose Time Critical only for OTP. Choose Realtime for immediate event-driven messages, or Batch for scheduled/batch messages.
answer_guidance.assistant_hint: This field affects router recommendation and traffic percentage strategy, especially for SMS.

fields.business_description: Department head, MD or GCB 2.

answer_guidance.business_hint: Provide the department head. This may be derived from department access information.
answer_guidance.assistant_hint: Prefer prefill from user profile or department information; ask in final review if missing.

fields.business_description: Team head, GCB 3.

answer_guidance.business_hint: Provide the team head.
answer_guidance.assistant_hint: Prefer prefill from user profile or department information; ask in final review if missing.

fields.business_description: Message owner, GCB 5 or above.

answer_guidance.business_hint: Provide the business owner responsible for the message, GCB 5 or above.
answer_guidance.assistant_hint: Important for governance; ask after main business/channel decisions if not prefilled.

fields.business_description: Business line first level, the department of the department head.

answer_guidance.business_hint: Provide the first-level business line / department of the department head.
answer_guidance.assistant_hint: Prefer prefill from department access information.

fields.business_description: Business line second level, the department of the team head.

answer_guidance.business_hint: Provide the second-level business line / department of the team head.
answer_guidance.assistant_hint: Prefer prefill from department access information.

fields.business_description: Whether delivery schedule supports 7*24.

answer_guidance.business_hint: Answer Y if the message delivery should support 7*24 operation; otherwise answer N.
answer_guidance.assistant_hint: Ask together with delivery mode when possible.

fields.business_description: High risk critical message flag. Examples: regulatory message, time-sensitive message like Card-Not-Present message, and message for high-risk transaction.

answer_guidance.business_hint: Answer Y if this is a regulatory, time-sensitive, or high-risk transaction message. Otherwise answer N.
answer_guidance.assistant_hint: This affects router/vendor and traffic percentage recommendation, especially SMS.

fields.business_description: Message trigger conditions.

answer_guidance.business_hint: Describe the business event or condition that triggers the message.
answer_guidance.assistant_hint: Even though optional in metadata, it is important for AC generation; extract from requirement and ask only if unclear.

example_answers: When a wealth product will mature soon
When a card-not-present transaction is detected

fields.business_description: Selected delivery channel. Channel is represented through channel rule list and priority, not as a simple flat field.

answer_guidance.business_hint: Select one or more channels such as EMAIL, SMS, PUSH, LETTER, WHATSAPP, WECHAT, MMS, or TWOWAYSMS.
answer_guidance.assistant_hint: Ask this early. Downstream router, sender, opt-in and bounce-back fields depend on it.

example_answers: PUSH first, then SMS as fallback

fields.business_description: Channel priority / mandatory routing. For channel delivery priority, values 1-8 indicate sending priority; value 0 indicates mandatory send; value 99 indicates optional / may not send.

answer_guidance.business_hint: Use 0 for mandatory send, 1-8 for channel priority order, and 99 for optional / may not send.
answer_guidance.assistant_hint: When user says one channel only, default priority can usually be 0 or ask for confirmation depending policy.

fields.business_description: Router / vendor path.
fields.raw_depends_on:
selected channel.
PUSH depends on app_name.
SMS depends on telecom / delivery_mode.
Email depends on internal/external setup.
Letter depends on auto/manual trigger.
2WAYSMS chooses HTCL_2WAY_SMS.

answer_guidance.business_hint: Choose the router/vendor path according to channel and business rule. New use cases usually avoid legacy ICCM/HUTCHISON routers unless reference use case requires them.
answer_guidance.assistant_hint: Recommend router automatically based on channel, app_name, delivery_mode, high_risk_flag, Send_to_China_flag, and internal/external context; ask user to confirm if uncertain.

fields.business_description: Router tag.

answer_guidance.business_hint: For SMS, tag may be HK or CN. For PUSH with DaaSC, tag may be IOS or AOS.
answer_guidance.assistant_hint: Only ask when tag is applicable based on channel/app/router; otherwise hide it.

fields.business_description: Requires to send to China Mobile Number or NOT.

answer_guidance.business_hint: Answer Y if SMS needs to be sent to China mobile numbers. This is used to consider adding CN routes.
answer_guidance.assistant_hint: If Y, recommend CM and LX routes with CM 100% and LX 0% traffic split.

example_answers: Y - need to send to China mobile numbers

fields.business_description: Traffic split percentage. Normally: high-risk real-time message: HTCL 100%, CSL 0% for resilience; Time Critical one-time-password: HTCL 70%, CSL 30% for resilience; real-time / batch message: HTCL 100%.
fields.required_level: Required for SMS/EMAIL multi-path; PUSH DAASC path can show it

answer_guidance.business_hint: Provide traffic split percentage when multiple paths/vendors are used. Examples: HTCL 100%, CSL 0%; HTCL 70%, CSL 30%; CM 100%, LX 0%.
answer_guidance.assistant_hint: Prefer default recommendation based on high_risk_flag, delivery_mode, Send_to_China_flag and selected routers; ask user to confirm rather than asking for raw number first.

fields.business_description: Sender ID / address / name depending on channel. For SMS, this is the sender ID number. For EMAIL, this is mainly the sender domain or sender address; only the bold/self-identifiable part can be customized, for example **hangseng**@mail.test.hangseng.com.
fields.raw_depends_on:
SMS - sender id number.
EMAIL - sender domain; only the bold/self-identifiable place can be customized, e.g. hangseng@mail.test.hangseng.com.

answer_guidance.business_hint: For SMS, provide the sender ID number/name. For EMAIL, provide the sender domain/address; only the self-identifiable part such as 'hangseng' in hangseng@mail.test.hangseng.com can be customized.
answer_guidance.assistant_hint: When channel is EMAIL, guide the user with the email-domain example. When channel is SMS, ask for SMS sender ID.

fields.business_description: Email sender name.
fields.raw_depends_on: IF EMAIL router contains PFP then this field is required. It can self-identify, but most HASE cases use 恒生银行 / Hang Seng Bank.

answer_guidance.business_hint: For Email, provide the sender display name. If the email router contains PFP, this field is required. Most HASE cases use 恒生银行 / Hang Seng Bank.
answer_guidance.assistant_hint: If router contains PFP, ask explicitly. Otherwise suggest default 恒生银行 / Hang Seng Bank and let user confirm.

fields.business_description: The App name for Push Notification.

answer_guidance.business_hint: Choose the app name for Push Notification: DaaSC, InvestEx, or DBB.
answer_guidance.assistant_hint: Ask only when PUSH is selected. App name controls PUSH router and tag.

fields.business_description: SMS cost center id.
fields.raw_depends_on: 要发 SMS 才要填. Example: 25267613

answer_guidance.business_hint: Provide SMS cost center id only when SMS is selected. Example: 25267613.
answer_guidance.assistant_hint: Ask only for SMS channel.

fields.business_description: Opt-in master flag. If SMS / MMS / EMAIL / WECHAT / PUSH channel is selected, this flag is Y; otherwise it is N. The raw field name is push_optin_flag, but the business description appears to be a master opt-in flag.
fields.raw_depends_on: If selected channel includes SMS/MMS/EMAIL/WECHAT/PUSH then Y, otherwise N. PUSH Channel if selected then must open.

answer_guidance.business_hint: Usually generated by selected channel. If SMS/MMS/EMAIL/WECHAT/PUSH is selected, it should be Y; otherwise N.
answer_guidance.assistant_hint: Do not ask user unless manual override is required. Show as generated/derived.

notes: Confirm with business whether raw field name push_optin_flag should be treated as master opt-in flag or push-only opt-in flag.

fields.business_description: Bounce back callback flag. Mobile will call MDC API to let MDC know when it received the PN, and MDC can bounce back to trigger next channel if system cannot get the API callback after a pre-defined period, such as 15 minutes.

answer_guidance.business_hint: Answer Y if mobile callback / bounce-back is required for Push Notification. It allows MDC to trigger next channel if callback is not received after a preset period.
answer_guidance.assistant_hint: Ask when PUSH is selected. Use plain wording: whether MDC should trigger fallback if mobile callback is not received.

example_answers: Y - enable callback and bounce back

fields.business_description: Letter bounce back success flag. It is for the INHK eStatement/eAdvice case, which triggers e-Notification when the eStatement/eAdvice is ready in website.

answer_guidance.business_hint: Use this for INHK eStatement/eAdvice cases where e-Notification is triggered when the eStatement/eAdvice is ready on website.
answer_guidance.assistant_hint: Ask only if LETTER or eStatement/eAdvice scenario is detected.

fields.business_description: Push bounce back period in minutes. It will trigger bounce back to next channel if there is no callback from mobile for the PN after this pre-set period.

answer_guidance.business_hint: Provide timeout in minutes for Push callback before triggering bounce back to next channel.
answer_guidance.assistant_hint: Ask only when PUSH is selected and bounce_back = Y. Example period from business description: 15 minutes.

fields.business_description: SMS bounce back period in minutes. It will trigger bounce back to next channel if MDC has not gotten the successful SENT status for SMS after this pre-set period.

answer_guidance.business_hint: Provide timeout in minutes before treating SMS as not successfully SENT and triggering next channel.
answer_guidance.assistant_hint: Ask only if SMS is selected and unknown bounce-back handling is enabled.

fields.business_description: Email bounce back period in minutes. It will trigger bounce back to next channel if MDC has not gotten the successful SENT status for EMAIL after this pre-set period.

answer_guidance.business_hint: Provide timeout in minutes before treating Email as not successfully SENT and triggering next channel.
answer_guidance.assistant_hint: Ask only if EMAIL is selected and unknown bounce-back handling is enabled.

fields.business_description: Letter bounce back period in minutes. It will trigger bounce back to next channel if MDC has not gotten the successful FAILED status for LETTER after this pre-set period.

answer_guidance.business_hint: Provide timeout in minutes. For LETTER, the business description refers to successful FAILED status, which differs from SMS/EMAIL successful SENT status.
answer_guidance.assistant_hint: Preserve the LETTER-specific wording; do not simplify it into SENT status.

fields.business_description: Wechat bounce back period in minutes.

answer_guidance.business_hint: Provide Wechat bounce back period in minutes when Wechat fallback handling is enabled.
answer_guidance.assistant_hint: Ask only if WECHAT is selected and unknown bounce-back handling is enabled.

fields.business_description: Whether route to next channel after bounce back, when the status is failed for the related channel delivery.

answer_guidance.business_hint: Answer Y if MDC should route to the next channel after bounce back / failed delivery status.
answer_guidance.assistant_hint: If Y, ask or derive unknown_bounce_back_status and channel-specific bounce back periods.

fields.business_description: When opened, MDC will check the channel bounce back period set above and bounce back to trigger next channel if it has not gotten the successful SENT status.

answer_guidance.business_hint: Choose whether unknown status should be treated as failed or successful. This controls whether MDC triggers the next channel when successful SENT status is not received.
answer_guidance.assistant_hint: Ask only when bounce_back_next_channel = Y.
